references.creationOrder: Create references/<reference>.md files before references/README.md when individual references are used; create references/README.md with an explicit zero-reference statement when none are used.
references.rule: Individual reference files are optional only when references/README.md explicitly states that no references were used or researched. Every listed reference file must exist, and every individual reference file must be listed with its references/<reference>.md path.

precedence.decision: PRODUCT_DECISIONS.md supersedes older narrative text only when the decision is explicit, dated or otherwise clearly recorded.
precedence.blockingQuestionRule: OPEN_QUESTIONS.md with open or deferred release-blocking questions blocks completion regardless of file precedence.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Map the product spec tree, document responsibilities and source-of-truth rules.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not contain detailed product rules, feature lists, roadmap content, release scope or technical decisions.
hardRules: Create before writing any other product spec file.
Do not create ad hoc product spec files outside the documented tree unless config explicitly allows them.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Catalog raw material used for shaping.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not interpret the product, define scope, synthesize features or make product decisions.
hardRules: No decision-relevant product idea or generated requirement may appear in final specs unless traceable to a source, reference or product decision.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Turn raw input into initial product understanding.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not list detailed features, define releases, close domain behavior or decide technical stack.
hardRules: Stop before roadmap or release scope if problem, user and expected value are unclear.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Keep uncertainty visible and separate from closed decisions.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not become a backlog or long-form discussion log.
hardRules: Questions affecting release scope, domain behavior, acceptance criteria or deliverables must be listed and not resolved silently.
Open or deferred questions with Blocks target release: yes block completion.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Map researched references and why each reference matters.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not consolidate all features, replace reference files or act as feature catalog.
hardRules: Every individual reference file must be listed with focus, relevance and its references/<reference>.md path.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Define the product at macro level.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not contain detailed feature catalog, roadmap, release scope, domain rules, acceptance criteria or technical decisions.
hardRules: Later contradictions must stop until repaired or explicitly superseded by product decision.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Centralize possible product capabilities from idea, references and decisions.
requiredFiles.limits: Is not a backlog, roadmap, release scope or delivery promise.
hardRules: Every feature must have exactly one valid feature status.
future, rejected or needs-decision features cannot enter SCOPE.md without explicit product decision.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Organize product evolution by conceptual priority and dependency.
requiredFiles.limits: Is not a calendar, sprint plan, timeline or closed delivery commitment.
hardRules: Roadmap defines relative sequence and conceptual dependencies only.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Describe conceptual business entities, relationships and modeling principles.
requiredFiles.limits: Is not a database schema, migration plan, ORM model or technical naming glossary.
hardRules: Names are business names; final technical names belong to technical shaping.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Describe target operational flow end to end.
requiredFiles.limits: Does not close release scope by itself.
hardRules: Any operational step outside the target release must be marked future or out of release scope.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Record closed product decisions, assumptions, risks and impacts.
requiredFiles.limits: Is not a changelog, backlog or full scope document.
createdAfter: can be created whenever decisions appear
hardRules: Accepted decisions that change scope, behavior, priority, tradeoff or direction must be recorded.
Proposed unresolved decisions belong in OPEN_QUESTIONS.md.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Explain release organization and relationship between release-level files.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not define detailed scope or behavior for one release.
hardRules: Custom release paths must come from config, not hardcoded assumptions.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Index one release and declare precedence for release documents.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not replace SCOPE.md, DOMAIN_SPEC.md, ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA.md or DELIVERABLES.md.
hardRules: Must declare which file owns release scope, domain behavior, acceptance and implementation handoff.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Define release commitment, included scope, excluded scope, tradeoffs and boundaries.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not define all detailed domain rules, task specs or technical stack.
hardRules: Nothing may enter domain spec, acceptance criteria or deliverables if outside SCOPE.md without explicit pending scope change.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Close release domain behavior and rules.
requiredFiles.limits: Is not a data contract, UI spec, task plan or stack decision document.
hardRules: Unresolved domain rules cannot become implementable deliverables without a human gate.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Turn scope and domain behavior into verifiable release readiness criteria.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not redefine scope or introduce domain rules absent from DOMAIN_SPEC.md or PRODUCT_DECISIONS.md.
hardRules: Every criterion must be testable, observable or objectively verifiable.

requiredFiles.responsibility: Convert the release into ordered, small, verifiable deliverables ready for delivery implementation.
requiredFiles.limits: Must not contain task specs, code, detailed implementation plans or task-level TDD requirements.
hardRules: Every deliverable must have exactly one valid deliverable status.
Only ready deliverables may enter delivery implementation without an additional human gate.
Do not include task specs.
